claim_audit_results.claim_summary: No specific budget figures (person-months, costs) cited in proposal text; deferred to validated budget tables
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase7_budget_gate/budget_gate_assessment.json — gate_pass_declaration: 'pass'; wp_coverage_results and partner_coverage_results contain structural coverage data only (present_in_budget: true, budget_line_reference) without specific person-month or cost figures. Proposal text correctly states 'Specific person-month figures per participant per WP are recorded in Table 3.1f in the Part A budget tables, consistent with the validated budget' — deferring to budget tables rather than citing figures, consistent with CLAUDE.md §8.3/8.4 constraint against fabricating or citing unvalidated budget figures.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Ethics flags triggered: Category 8 (AI-based systems in safety-relevant domains), Category 4 (special category health data), Category 2 (human participants in clinical validation context)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json — ethics_assessment.issues: ETHICS-01 (category: 'Category 8 — Artificial Intelligence'), ETHICS-02 (category: 'Category 4 — Personal Data (Special Category: Health Data)'), ETHICS-03 (category: 'Category 2 — Human Participants (Medical Study Context)'); ethics_issues_identified: true. All three categories match the section's claim.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Contributing partner lists per WP derived from wp_structure.json (Tier 4) rather than roles.json (Tier 3) where discrepancies exist for ISIA, NIHS, ELI in non-lead WPs
claim_audit_results.source_ref: CLAUDE.md §3 authority hierarchy (Tier 4 governs Tier 3 when conflict exists). Tier 4 source: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json partner_role_matrix wps_as_contributor per partner. Tier 3 source: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/roles.json wp_participant per partner. Discrepancies verified: ISIA — roles.json wp_participant: ['WP2','WP4','WP8','WP9'] vs wp_structure.json wps_as_contributor: ['WP1','WP8','WP9']; NIHS — roles.json: ['WP2','WP3','WP8'] vs wp_structure.json: ['WP1','WP8']; ELI — roles.json: ['WP4','WP8','WP9'] vs wp_structure.json: ['WP1','WP8','WP9']. Derivation chain: Tier 4 phase3 artifact is the downstream-authoritative source for WP design; section correctly uses wp_structure.json contributing_partners values. Inference is traceable and the reasoning is stateable.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: The consortium spans four organisation types (higher education establishments, research institutes, and private companies)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json — organisation_type field values present: HES (ATU, BIIS, ISIA, NIHS), REC (CERIA, FIIT), PRC (BAL, ELI). Only 3 distinct organisation_type values in the source.
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: The proposal text asserts 'four organisation types' but: (1) docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json contains exactly 3 distinct organisation_type values (HES, REC, PRC) across all 8 partners; (2) the proposal text itself enumerates only three categories ('higher education establishments', 'research institutes', and 'private companies'), making the count of 'four' internally self-contradictory as well as contradicted by the Tier 3 source. No fourth organisation type is evidenced in any Tier 1–4 artifact. Source contradiction: Tier 3 docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json shows 3 distinct org_type codes, not 4.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Each partner leads exactly one work package, ensuring unambiguous accountability and non-overlapping leadership responsibilities
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: The proposal text states 'each partner leads exactly one work package' (stated twice in B.3.2) but Tier 4 source docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json partner_role_matrix shows ATU wps_as_lead: ['WP1','WP2'] — ATU leads two work packages (WP1: Project Management and Coordination; WP2: Neuro-Symbolic Planning and Reasoning Engine). Seven of eight partners lead exactly one WP, but ATU leads two. This claim is a project-fact assertion about WP leadership structure that is directly contradicted by the named Tier 4 source artifact. Additionally the claim in the final sentence of B.3.2 ('each of the eight partners leads exactly one work package') repeats this unresolved discrepancy.
